generatedSentence: Jeepers, the normally vibrant weekend outlook of the jaded cartographer was literally eaten by a sudden layoff, an inhuman blow to his important geography job, nonetheless, a blackguard of pity; the humidity seemed to infect his inward spirit, making his normally cheerful outlook ruffled. His vision, once clear, became vague and occluded by the memory of the blackthorn tree, a rusty noise from a faraway network, a negative jinx upon his hepatic trial, feeling like a lumpy mouse caught in the teacher's nasty accounting web; the ugly application of jobbery and jobs was a terrible promise, a true disaster. The resourceful native, a whistler with a waterproof jacket and a trusty jimmy, knew this boundary of urban impiety was important; the gangsta weasel, dressed in a vandyke, had hidden the kiloton of jade, hoping to dulcify the situation, but the woodpecker's raucous noise and a canary's catchy song revealed the awful truth. With a wild embrace, the dromedary, carrying the hopeful cell of funding, unlocked the mystery, revealing the pantheons of justice; the blackguard's baggage was replevied, his vision, once afflicted by pinkeye, cleared, and the theatre's impact, a joyful volume of tissue, proved death’s promise reliable, ending the messy noise of conflict.
panelSentences: Jeepers, the normally vibrant weekend outlook of the jaded cartographer was literally eaten by a sudden layoff, an inhuman blow to his important geography job, nonetheless, a blackguard of pity; the humidity seemed to infect his inward spirit, making his normally cheerful outlook ruffled.

His vision, once clear, became vague and occluded by the memory of the blackthorn tree, a rusty noise from a faraway network, a negative jinx upon his hepatic trial, feeling like a lumpy mouse caught in the teacher's nasty accounting web; the ugly application of jobbery and jobs was a terrible promise, a true disaster.

The resourceful native, a whistler with a waterproof jacket and a trusty jimmy, knew this boundary of urban impiety was important; the gangsta weasel, dressed in a vandyke, had hidden the kiloton of jade, hoping to dulcify the situation, but the woodpecker's raucous noise and a canary's catchy song revealed the awful truth.

With a wild embrace, the dromedary, carrying the hopeful cell of funding, unlocked the mystery, revealing the pantheons of justice; the blackguard's baggage was replevied, his vision, once afflicted by pinkeye, cleared, and the theatre's impact, a joyful volume of tissue, proved death’s promise reliable, ending the messy noise of conflict.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone, jaded cartographer, wearing a worn leather jacket with a futuristic headset and a scuffed, waterproof backpack. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The cartographer sits at a holographic desk, head in hands, layoff notice glowing ominously, surrounded by ghostly geographic projections, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Close-up on the cartographer's face, eyes wide with a mix of dread and resolve, a faint pinkeye visible, with abstract, lumpy, jinx-like shadows swirling around, high angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The cartographer, now disguised as a resourceful native, jimmying open a hidden compartment in a gnarled blackthorn tree, a wild canary perched nearby, with cinematic lighting emphasizing the jade within, low angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The cartographer, now triumphant, stands beside a large dromedary carrying a futuristic data cell, a bright, hopeful glow emanating from it, the cityscape in the background now appearing clear and vibrant, full shot, bold ink outlines.

definitions.definition: Nevertheless.
A description of the earth: a treatise or textbook on geography; an atlas or gazetteer.
The study of the physical properties of the earth, including how humans affect and are affected by them.
Terrain: the physical properties of a region of the earth.
Any subject considered in terms of its physical distribution.
Similar books, studies, or regions concerning other planets.
The physical arrangement of any place, particularly a house.
The lavatory: a room used for urination and defecation.
The relative arrangement of the parts of anything.
A territory: a geographical area as a field of business or market sector.
To roll or fold up; to wind round; to entwine.
To envelop completely; to surround; to cover; to hide.
definitions.example: to involve in darkness or obscurity

definitions.definition: To complicate or make intricate, as in grammatical structure.
To connect with something as a natural or logical consequence or effect; to include necessarily; to imply.
To take in; to gather in; to mingle confusedly; to blend or merge.
To envelop, enfold, entangle.
definitions.example: He's involved in the crime.

definitions.definition: To engage (someone) to participate in a task.
definitions.example: By getting involved in her local community, Mary met lots of people and also helped make it a nicer place to live.

definitions.definition: To raise to any assigned power; to multiply, as a quantity, into itself a given number of times.
definitions.example: a quantity involved to the third or fourth power

definitions.definition: Complicated.
definitions.example: He related an involved story about every ancestor since 1895.

definitions.definition: Associated with others, be a participant or make someone be a participant (in a crime, process, etc.)
definitions.example: He got involved in a bar fight.

definitions.definition: Having an affair with someone.
Of or pertaining to inhumanity and the indifferently cruel, sadistic or barbaric behavior it brings.
(often pluralized) The alimentary canal of an animal through which food passes after having passed all stomachs.
One of certain subdivisions of this part of the alimentary canal, such as the small or large intestine in human beings.
To tire, weary or fatigue
To treat like a jade; to spurn.
To make ridiculous and contemptible.
Bored or lacking enthusiasm, typically after having been over exposed to, or having consumed too much of something.
Worn out, wearied, exhausted or lacking enthusiasm, due to age or experience.
Made callous or cynically insensitive, by experience.
A feeling of sympathy at the misfortune or suffering of someone or something.
definitions.example: I can't feel any pity towards the gang, who got injured while attempting to break into a flat.

definitions.definition: Something regrettable.
definitions.example: It's a pity you're feeling unwell because there's a party on tonight.

definitions.definition: Piety.
To feel pity for (someone or something).
definitions.example: You have got to pity the guy - he lost his wife, mother and job in the same month.

definitions.definition: To make (someone) feel pity; to provoke the sympathy or compassion of.
Short form of what a pity.
Dampness, especially that of the air.
The amount of water vapour in the air.
Pulsing with energy or activity.
definitions.example: He has a vibrant personality.

definitions.definition: Lively and vigorous.
Vibrating, resonant or resounding.
(of a colour) Bright.
To make a ruffle in; to curl or flute, as an edge of fabric.
definitions.example: Ruffle the end of the cuff.

definitions.definition: To disturb; especially, to cause to flutter.
definitions.example: Her sudden volley of insults ruffled his composure.

definitions.definition: To grow rough, boisterous, or turbulent.
To become disordered; to play loosely; to flutter.
To be rough; to jar; to be in contention; hence, to put on airs; to swagger.
To make into a ruff; to draw or contract into puckers, plaits, or folds; to wrinkle.
To erect in a ruff, as feathers.
To beat with the ruff or ruffle, as a drum.
To throw together in a disorderly manner.
Having ruffles.
definitions.example: It would have slid easily across the floor if not for the ruffled undersurface causing friction.

definitions.definition: Puffed up like a bird's feathers.
Bothered.
(chiefly US) A dismissal of employees from their jobs because of tightened budgetary constraints or work shortage (not due to poor performance or misconduct).
A period of time when someone is unavailable for work.
A short pass that has been rolled in front of another player for them to kick.
Any of a group of European plants, genus, Lavandula, of the mint family.
A pale purple colour, like that of the lavender flower.
To decorate or perfume with lavender.
Having a pale purple colour.
Pertaining to LGBT people and rights
Pertaining to lesbian feminism; opposing heterosexism.
(old-fashioned, usually used only of men) A scoundrel; an unprincipled contemptible person; an untrustworthy person.
A man who uses foul language in front of a woman, typically a woman of high standing in society.
To revile or abuse in scurrilous language.
To act like a blackguard; to be a scoundrel.
Having relevant and crucial value.

definitions.definition: Pompous; self-important.
(chiefly in the plural) That which is inward or within; the inner parts or organs of the body; the viscera.
(chiefly in the plural) The mental faculties.
A familiar friend or acquaintance.
Situated on the inside; that is within, inner; belonging to the inside.
Intimate, closely acquainted; familiar.
Towards the inside.
Used to express surprise
A spicy salami-style Italian-American sausage.
Pizza with only tomato sauce, cheese and pepperoni toppings.

definitions.definition: A pair of headphones or earphones, or a singular headphone or earphone, typically with an attached microphone.
Any electronic device worn on the head.
On a bicycle, the system of bearings that connects the fork to the frame.
A place from which something can be viewed.

definitions.definition: Expectation for the future.

To use up.

definitions.definition: To take the loss in a transaction.
To be injured or killed by (something such as a firearm or its projectile), especially in the mouth.
To corrode or erode.

definitions.definition: To perform oral sex (on a person or body part).

definitions.definition: (especially in combination) That has been consumed by eating
Offbeat, unconventional or eccentric.
Not quite right; of questionable quality; not appropriate to the context.
Cool; great; excellent.

Relating to or reminiscent of various genres of African American music, especially funk.
Relating to, or characterized by, great fear, or funking.
The break in the working week, usually two days including the traditional holy or sabbath day. Thus in western countries, Saturday and Sunday.

definitions.definition: To bring into contact with a substance that causes illness (a pathogen).

definitions.definition: Infected.

The juicy aggregate fruit of these plants.
A red colour, the colour of a ripe raspberry.
To gather or forage for raspberries.

A cripple.
To make the noise intended to imitate the passing of flatulence.
Word for word; not figuratively; not as an idiom or metaphor

definitions.definition: (degree, contranym) Used non-literally as an intensifier for figurative statements: virtually, so to speak (often considered incorrect; see usage notes)
definitions.example: He was so surprised, he literally jumped twenty feet in the air.

definitions.definition: Used to intensify or dramatise non-figurative statements; tending towards a meaningless filler word in repeated use.
definitions.example: I had no idea, so I was literally guessing.

definitions.definition: Used as a generic downtoner: just, merely.
definitions.example: You literally put it in the microwave for five minutes and it's done.

Made of or covered with material that doesn't allow water in.

Type 92 heavy machine gun

(automobile insurance) An overturn.

definitions.definition: An upset stomach.
An upper set; a subset (X,≤) of a partially ordered set with the property that, if x is in U and x≤y, then y is in U.
The dangerous situation where the flight attitude or airspeed of an aircraft is outside the designed bounds of operation, possibly resulting in loss of control.
To make (a person) angry, distressed, or unhappy.

definitions.definition: To disturb, disrupt or adversely alter (something).
definitions.example: Introducing a foreign species can upset the ecological balance.

definitions.definition: To tip or overturn (something).
To defeat unexpectedly.

definitions.definition: To be upset or knocked over.
definitions.example: The carriage upset when the horse bolted.

definitions.definition: To set up; to put upright.
To thicken and shorten, as a heated piece of iron, by hammering on the end.
To shorten (a tire) in the process of resetting, originally by cutting it and hammering on the ends.
(of a person) Angry, distressed or unhappy.

definitions.definition: (of a stomach or gastrointestinal tract, referred to as stomach) Feeling unwell, nauseated, or ready to vomit.
definitions.example: His stomach was upset, so he didn't want to move.

definitions.definition: A large shrub or small tree, Prunus spinosa, that is native to Europe, western Asia, and north Africa. It has a dark bark and bears thorns.
A stick or staff taken from this tree.
Feeling or causing joy.
To fit out with the necessary clothing; to clothe, put clothes on (something or someone).
definitions.example: He was dressed in the latest fashions.

definitions.definition: To clothe oneself; to put on clothes.
definitions.example: I rose and dressed before daybreak.  It's very cold out. Dress warm.

definitions.definition: To put on the uniform and equipment necessary to play the game.
definitions.example: Due to a left ankle sprain, Kobe Bryant did not dress for the game against Indiana

definitions.definition: Of a man, to allow the genitals to fall to one side or other within the trousers.
definitions.example: Does sir dress to the right or the left?

definitions.definition: To prepare (food) for cooking, especially by seasoning it.
To prepare oneself; to make ready.
To adorn, ornament.

definitions.definition: To ornament (a ship) by hoisting the national colours at the peak and mastheads, and setting the jack forward; when "dressed full", the signal flags and pennants are added.
To prepare (a set) by installing the props, scenery, etc.
To treat (a wound, or wounded person).
To prepare for use; to fit for any use; to render suitable for an intended purpose; to get ready.

definitions.definition: To prepare the surface of (a material; usually stone or lumber).
To manure (land).
To bolt or sift flour.
(sometimes imperative) To arrange in exact continuity of line, as soldiers; commonly to adjust to a straight line and at proper distance; to align.

definitions.definition: To break and train for use, as a horse or other animal.
Having been subjected to a preparatory process or treatment; treated, prepared.
Prepared for eating, especially by the addition of specific condiments or dressing.
Wearing clothes; attired (now often with qualifying word).
A highly contagious form of conjunctivitis.
To obstruct, cover, or otherwise block (an opening, a portion of an image, etc.).
To absorb, as a gas by a metal.
Dreadful; causing terror, alarm and fear; awesome
definitions.example: The witch gave him a terrible curse.

definitions.definition: Formidable, powerful.
Intense; extreme in degree or extent.
definitions.example: He paid a terrible price for his life of drinking.

definitions.definition: Unpleasant; disagreeable.
definitions.example: The food was terrible, but it was free.

definitions.definition: Very bad; lousy.
definitions.example: Whatever he thinks, he is a terrible driver.

definitions.definition: In a terrible way; to a terrible extent; terribly; awfully.
A wandering; a vagary.
An indefinite expanse.
To wander; to roam; to stray.
To become vague or act in a vague manner.
Not clearly expressed; stated in indefinite terms.
Not having a precise meaning.

definitions.definition: Not clearly defined, grasped, or understood; indistinct; slight.

definitions.definition: Not clearly felt or sensed; somewhat subconscious.

definitions.definition: Not thinking or expressing one’s thoughts clearly or precisely.
Lacking expression; vacant.
Not sharply outlined; hazy.
Wandering; vagrant; vagabond.
Replevin
To return goods to their rightful owner by replevin; to recover goods.
To bail.
A small, usually yellow, finch (genus Serinus), a songbird native to the Canary Islands.
Any of various small birds of different countries, most of which are largely yellow in colour.
A light, slightly greenish, yellow colour.
A light, sweet, white wine from the Canary Islands.
A lively dance, possibly of Spanish origin (also called canaries).
Any test subject, especially an inadvertent or unwilling one. (From the mining practice of using canaries to detect dangerous gases.)
A value placed in memory such that it will be the first data corrupted by a buffer overflow, allowing the program to identify and recover from it.
A female singer, soprano, a coloratura singer.
An informer or snitch; a squealer.
A (usually yellow) capsule of the short-acting barbiturate pentobarbital/pentobarbitone (Nembutal).
A yellow sticker of unroadworthiness.
To dance nimbly (as in the canary dance)
To inform or snitch, to betray secrets, especially about illegal activities.
Of a light yellow colour.
An aboriginal inhabitant of a region colonized by English-speaking people; in particular:
Aboriginal inhabitant of a region colonized by English-speaking people; in particular:
A person who is native to a place; a person who was born in a place.
A native speaker.
Ostrea edulis, a kind of oyster.
Belonging to one by birth.
definitions.example: English is not my native language.

definitions.definition: Characteristic of or relating to people inhabiting a region from prehistoric times.
definitions.example: The native peoples of Australia are called aborigines.

definitions.definition: Born or grown in the region in which it lives or is found; not foreign or imported.
definitions.example: Many native artists studied abroad.

definitions.definition: (of a species) Which occurs of its own accord in a given locality, to be contrasted with a species introduced by man.
definitions.example: The naturalized Norway maple often outcompetes the native North American sugar maple.

definitions.definition: (of software) Pertaining to the system or architecture in question.

definitions.definition: Occurring naturally in its pure or uncombined form; native aluminium, native salt.
Arising by birth; having an origin; born.
Original; constituting the original substance of anything.

definitions.definition: Naturally related; cognate; connected (with).
A large bird, Aramus guarauna, found in marshes in the Caribbean, Central America and southern Florida.
Wholly, entirely.
(focus) Without any other reason etc.; only, just, and nothing more.
Someone or something that whistles, or who plays a whistle as a musical instrument.
Any of several passerine birds of the genus Pachycephala, of Australasia and the western Pacific.
Any bird that whistles or is noted for its whistling vocalisations (applied regionally to various specific species).
The goldeneye (certain ducks of genus Bucephala).
The whistling marmot (Marmota caligata).
The mountain beaver (Aplodontia rufa).
An audio-frequency electromagnetic wave produced by atmospheric disturbances such as lightning.
A broken-winded horse.
The keeper of a whistling shop, or shebeen.
Related to the (or any) city.
Characteristic of city life.
Relating to contemporary African American culture.
The improper or corrupt act or conduct of public or official business for the sake of private gain.
A task.

definitions.definition: An economic role for which a person is paid.

definitions.definition: (in noun compounds) Plastic surgery.

definitions.definition: A task, or series of tasks, carried out in batch mode (especially on a mainframe computer).

Any affair or event which affects one, whether fortunately or unfortunately.
A thing (often used in a vague way to refer to something whose name one cannot recall).

definitions.definition: To do odd jobs or occasional work for hire.
To work as a jobber.
To take the loss.
To buy and sell for profit, as securities; to speculate in.

definitions.definition: To seek private gain under pretence of public service; to turn public matters to private advantage.

definitions.definition: A measure of the strength of an explosion or a bomb based on how many thousand tons of TNT would be needed to produce the same energy.

definitions.definition: Material (such as clothing, makeup, a wig) used to alter one’s visual appearance in order to hide one's identity or assume another.

definitions.definition: The appearance of something on the outside which masks what's beneath.

definitions.definition: A change of behaviour resulting from intoxication.
To change the appearance of (a person or thing) so as to hide, or to assume an identity.

definitions.definition: To avoid giving away or revealing (something secret); to hide by a false appearance.

definitions.definition: To affect or change by liquor; to intoxicate.
The cessation of life and all associated processes; the end of an organism's existence as an entity independent from its environment and its return to an inert, nonliving state.
definitions.example: The death of my grandmother saddened the whole family.

definitions.definition: (often capitalized) The personification of death as a hooded figure with a scythe; the Grim Reaper. The pronoun he is not the only option, but probably the most traditional one, as it matches with the male grammatical gender of Old English dēaþ, also with cognate German der Tod. The fourth apocalyptic rider (Bible, revelations 6:8) is male θᾰ́νᾰτος (thanatos) in Greek. It has the female name Mors in Latin, but is referred to with male forms qui and eum. The following quotes show this rider on a pale horse is his in the English Bible and she in Peter Gabriel's lyrics.
definitions.example: When death walked in, a chill spread through the room.

definitions.definition: (the death) The collapse or end of something.
definitions.example: England scored a goal at the death to even the score at one all.

definitions.definition: Spiritual lifelessness.
To attempt to stop the progression of; to resist or antagonize by physical means, or by arguments, etc.; to contend against.

definitions.definition: To object to.

definitions.definition: To present or set up in opposition; to pose.

Being, of having an opponent; not unopposed.
Opposite.

To form a debt into a stock charged with interest.
The action of the verb fund.
Money provided as funds.

definitions.definition: The least weasel, Mustela nivalis.

A devious or sneaky person or animal.

A type of yarn winder used for counting the yardage of handspun yarn. It most commonly has a wooden peg or dowel that pops up from the gearing mechanism after a certain number of yards have been wound onto the winder.
To achieve by clever or devious means.
To gain something for oneself by clever or devious means.
To engage in clever or devious behavior.
The act of unlocking something.

definitions.definition: To be or become unfastened or unrestrained.

Of a grayish shade of green, typical of jade stones.

Full acceptance (of something).

To encircle; to enclose, to encompass.

definitions.definition: To fasten on, as armour.

definitions.definition: A room in a prison or jail for one or more inmates.

definitions.definition: From or relating to wild creatures.

definitions.definition: Unrestrained or uninhibited.

definitions.definition: Raucous, unruly, or licentious.
definitions.example: The fraternity was infamous for its wild parties, which frequently resulted in police involvement.

definitions.definition: Visibly and overtly anxious; frantic.

definitions.definition: Disheveled, tangled, or untidy.

definitions.definition: Enthusiastic.
definitions.example: I'm not wild about the idea of a two day car trip with my nephews, but it's my only option.

definitions.definition: Inaccurate.

definitions.definition: Exposed to the wind and sea; unsheltered.

definitions.definition: Hard to steer; said of a vessel.
(of a knot) Not capable of being represented as a finite closed polygonal chain.
Amazing, awesome, unbelievable.

definitions.definition: Able to stand in for others, e.g. a card in games, or a text character in computer pattern matching.
definitions.example: In this card game, aces are wild: they can take the place of any other card.

definitions.definition: Inaccurately; not on target.
definitions.example: The javelin flew wild and struck a spectator, to the horror of all observing.

definitions.definition: (usually uncountable) Luggage; traveling equipment
definitions.example: Please put your baggage in the trunk.

definitions.definition: Factors, especially psychological ones, which interfere with a person's ability to function effectively.

definitions.definition: A woman.
An army's portable equipment; its baggage train.
The striking of one body against another; collision.

definitions.definition: A forced impinging.

definitions.definition: A significant or strong influence; an effect.

definitions.definition: To collide or strike, the act of impinging.

definitions.definition: To compress; to compact; to press into something or pack together.

definitions.definition: To influence; to affect; to have an impact on.

definitions.definition: To stamp or impress onto something.

definitions.definition: The state of being impious.

Any swift riding camel.

Thin, woven, gauze-like fabric.

Absorbent paper as material.
A group of cells similar in origin that function together to do a specific job.
Web; texture; complicated fabrication; connected series.

A three-dimensional measure of space that comprises a length, a width and a height. It is measured in units of cubic centimeters in metric, cubic inches or cubic feet in English measurement.

definitions.definition: Strength of sound; loudness.
